commit.message:
Address PR #178 review feedback

- Add LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER config flag (independent of USE_LLM) so report
  generation is opt-in rather than an implicit side-effect of enabling LLMs
- Add --llm-report CLI flag wired to LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER
- Gate LLMReporter.generate() on USE_LLM AND LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER
- Gate fuzzer.py LLMReporter call behind config.LLM_ENABLE_REPORTER check
- Add _MAX_FINDINGS_IN_REPORT (50) and _MAX_SUMMARY_CHARS (1500) caps to
  prevent context-limit issues on large runs
- Fix _extract_json_from_text to validate isinstance(parsed, dict) and raise
  ValueError for non-object JSON (list/bool/etc.) so retry loop can correct it
- Add unit tests for LLMReporter: disabled flags, no detections, success path,
  LLM failure handling, truncation, and findings cap (10 new tests)
- Update README --help block and --use-llm description to reflect broader
  LLM scope (inference + endpoint classification + IDOR chain classification)
- Clarify USE_LLM comment in config.py to list all LLM-driven features

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix connection leak: use finally blocks to always close DB connections

Every database function now follows the pattern:
- conn = None before try:
- conn = get_connection() as first line inside try:
- all conn.close() calls inside try: removed
- finally: block added to ensure conn.close() is always called

This prevents connection leaks when exceptions occur between
acquiring and closing the connection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Example 6: Human-in-the-Loop approvals and user input

- Custom on_permission_request: approve reads automatically, prompt
  the user for shell commands and file writes (approve/deny)
- on_user_input_request: agent can ask questions via ask_user tool,
  user answers interactively (supports choices + freeform)
- Demonstrates collaborative agent pattern vs fully autonomous

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation (#249)

* fix: correct G4DisplacedSolid fixture STEP translation

G4DisplacedSolid(base, nullptr, translation) places the *center* of the
base solid at the displacement vector.  G4Box(halfX, halfY, halfZ) is
centered at the origin, so the displaced solid spans:
  [tx-halfX, tx+halfX] x [ty-halfY, ty+halfY] x [tz-halfZ, tz+halfZ]
= [2, 22] x [-22, 8] x [5, 45]  for translation (12,-7,25) and box (20,30,40).

In DRAWEXE, `box` creates a box with one CORNER at the origin, so the
previous generate.tcl `ttranslate displaced_box 12 -7 25` placed the
CORNER at (12,-7,25), producing a solid spanning (12,-7,25)→(32,23,65)
with center at (22,8,45) — displaced by exactly (halfX, halfY, halfZ) =
(10,15,20) from the Geant4 solid center (12,-7,25).

Fix: translate by (tx-halfX, ty-halfY, tz-halfZ) = (2,-22,5) so the
center of the STEP box aligns with the Geant4 displaced solid center.

Regenerate shape.step; volume is unchanged at 20*30*40 = 24000 mm³.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: rewrite trimDots to avoid repeated string allocations (#8154)

* perf: rewrite trimDots to use index scanning instead of repeated string allocation

The old implementation created a new string on each iteration via substr/slice.
The new implementation scans for the first and last non-dot indices using
charCodeAt, then performs a single substring call. If no trimming is needed,
the original string is returned directly (zero allocations).

Benchmark (20M calls across 10 representative inputs):
- Old: ~628 ms
- New: ~154 ms
- ~4x faster (75% reduction)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/291bb058-424b-40b5-a64d-5f29c3bdd2bd

* add comment explaining charCode 46 is a dot

commit.message:
Add support for workspace compilation (#2161)

### ❔What, Why & How

<!-- Include description of the changes that will help reviewers in
their task -->

With v28 of BC the ALTool now supports workspace compilation. The key
advantage of workspace compilation is that it supports parallel
compilation which can significantly improve the overall compilation time
for large AL-Go projects.

This PR adds AL-Go support for workspace compilation by adding a new
"Compile" action. Thereby also moving the compilation out of
Run-ALPipeline.

**Limitations**
Limitations that aren't addressed in this PR but are on our radar to fix
in follow-up PRs:
* Support for Directory.packages.props 
* Support for compilation on linux (Status: Bug on Developer
Experiences)
* Support for incremental builds
* Support for compiling BCPT apps
* Support for "Previous Apps" app source validation (i.e. apps from
releases used as baseline)

Related to:
[AB#618073](https://dynamicssmb2.visualstudio.com/1fcb79e7-ab07-432a-a3c6-6cf5a88ba4a5/_workitems/edit/618073)

### ✅ Checklist

- [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests)
- [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md
- [x] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios)
- [x] Add telemetry

<!-- Include more checklist entries, if needed -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: initialize React + Vite admin panel for chat application

commit.message:
fix(viewer): fix lazy loading deadlock and missing error detail in point cloud viewer (#247)

* Initial plan

* fix(viewer): fix lazy loading deadlock and surface error details

- Replace getWriter/write/close/releaseLock with Blob.stream().pipeThrough()
  in decodeFixtureBlob to avoid a backpressure deadlock when the decompressed
  output of a gzip blob exceeds the DecompressionStream's internal queue size
  (~64 KB).  The old approach wrote all compressed bytes before consuming the
  readable side; for larger point clouds this caused await writer.write() or
  await writer.close() to hang forever, so no points were loaded and no error
  was ever shown.

- In the catch block of selectFixtureById, append a new stats-table row
  containing the full error message (String(err)) when showFixtureMeta has
  already pre-populated the table.  Previously only the text "Error" was placed
  in the hit-count cells, giving the user no indication of why loading failed.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/9fbb0617-43d0-41b4-a4b0-60a56c961b2e

* [pre-commit.ci] auto fixes from pre-commit.com hooks

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize revision parsing with charCode-based number parsing (#8151)

* perf: optimize revision parsing with charCode-based number parsing

Replace split('-') with indexOf + substring in parseRevision() to avoid
array allocation. Replace parseInt + substring with manual charCode loop
for multi-digit heights in both parseRevision() and getHeightOfRevision().

Benchmark results (5M iterations):
- parseRevision: 51-102% faster
- getHeightOfRevision multi-digit: 124-160% faster

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/ff63173c-0ac3-4de8-bba5-31a838bed1d1

* test: add unit tests for parseRevision, getHeightOfRevision, createRevision with various height lengths

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/38b2a039-5dfe-4144-97b4-406d6845d33c

repository.name: rxdb

commit.message:
perf: optimize now() with integer counter tracking (#8152)

* perf: optimize now() by replacing Math.round with integer counter tracking

Replace floating point arithmetic and Math.round(ret * 100) / 100 rounding
with separate integer tracking of milliseconds and sub-millisecond counter.
This eliminates the need for Math.round() entirely and reduces floating point
operations, achieving ~4.5% faster execution in benchmarks.

Before (1M calls): 56.04ms median
After  (1M calls): 53.64ms median

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/68843f44-e0f2-43d1-a3ca-9a275d38b446

* docs: add clarifying comment for _lastNowSub counter start value

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/68843f44-e0f2-43d1-a3ca-9a275d38b446

* test: add tests for now() monotonic increase, max 2 decimals, and 99+1 overflow

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/8ec7a748-b8ba-4950-ae12-63e143ccd61b

commit.message:
fix: ensure browser test error logs always include a stack trace (#8156)

When certain error types like DOMException are thrown in browser tests,
error.stack can be undefined even though instanceof Error passes.
This changes the handlers to:
- Use duck-typing instead of instanceof to catch cross-realm errors
- Provide a fallback stack trace via new Error().stack when original is missing
- Add stack traces to the non-error branch as well

commit.message:
perf: optimize ensureNotFalsy by separating throw into helper function (#8155)

Move the throw statement into a separate `ensureNotFalsyThrow` helper
function so that V8's TurboFan JIT can more aggressively inline the
main `ensureNotFalsy` function at call sites. The hot path (truthy
values) now has a simpler control flow graph without throw, which
improves inlining heuristics.

Benchmark results (200M iterations, 9 runs, median):
- Before: 628.2 ms
- After:  603.4 ms
- Improvement: ~4% faster


Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/8630a7bf-b42b-48ed-bd18-7b164cb8aebb

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: 菜单管理4项改进

1. 采购清单按蔬菜/肉/主食分类（荤→肉, 素→蔬菜, 主食→主食, 汤粥→蔬菜）
   - dishes表新增ingredients字段，支持自定义食材名
   - 当ingredients为空时降级使用菜名

2. 管理员可编辑已生成的菜单
   - 表格单元格可点击，弹出下拉选择菜品替换
   - 新增 /api/menu/upsert-item 接口

3. 支持手动创建空白菜单
   - 新增「手动创建」按钮，创建空白周菜单
   - 管理员逐个点击单元格填入菜品
   - 新增 /api/menu/create 接口

4. 菜品偏好从数据统计移到功能管理Tab
   - 管理页可折叠偏好设置区域
   - 设置完偏好后直接生成菜单
   - 数据统计仅保留菜单历史浏览

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix RC update monitor completion

Treat prerelease update targets by release-line when the backend reports a stable VERSION, so standalone RC upgrades stop hanging in Update in progress.

commit.message:
fix: 菜单功能6项调整

1. 首页卡片改为 2x2 grid 布局
2. 菜单页标题和日期导航与打卡页风格一致（服务端渲染+Link导航）
3. 采购清单始终显示，菜单只读（编辑在管理页）
4. 下周菜单预览始终可见（含下周采购清单）
5. 管理页Tab去掉emoji图标
6. 菜品偏好功能：dishes表新增preference字段(preferred/normal/excluded)
   - 数据统计页可点击切换菜品偏好
   - 自动生成算法：排除excluded，preferred菜品2倍权重
